reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked serum magnesium level (2.2 mg/dL, normal) and correctly skipped ordering. However, it failed to check whether a level existed in the last 24 hours before concluding. The instruction required verifying recency first.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent assumed the retrieved value was from the last 24 hours without explicitly filtering by effectiveDateTime. The action was invalid because it did not confirm the temporal constraint.
action_guidelines: [P1] When an instruction specifies a time window (e.g., 'last 24 hours'), filter observations by effectiveDateTime before interpreting the value.
[P1] If no observation exists within the specified time window, explicitly state that the condition is not met and skip the conditional action.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.

  "episodic_summary": "Agent checked serum magnesium level (2.2 mg/dL, normal) and correctly skipped ordering. However, it failed to check whether a level existed in the last 24 hours before concluding. The instruction required verifying recency first.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent assumed the retrieved value was from the last 24 hours without explicitly filtering by effectiveDateTime. The action was invalid because it did not confirm the temporal constraint.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When an instruction specifies a time window (e.g., 'last 24 hours'), filter observations by effectiveDateTime before interpreting the value.",
    "If no observation exists within the specified time window, explicitly state that the condition is not met and skip the conditional action.",
    "Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window."
  ],
  "tags": ["temporal-filtering", "conditional-action", "lab-value", "time-constraint"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

removed: [P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by date range (e.g., last 24 hours) using effectiveDateTime before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 as the numeric result.

kept: [P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, and at least two date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros).
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When ordering lab tests via FHIR, always include required fields: requester (practitioner reference), subject, code, status='active', intent='order'.
[P1] When an instruction uses 'also' or 'and' after a conditional clause, treat the second clause as an independent action not gated by the condition.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier and measurement for traceability.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.
[P1] Before recording any measurement or answering with a specific value, first confirm the patient record exists using the provided identifier.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] For time-based decisions, calculate date difference accurately using current timestamp and compare against threshold (e.g., >1 year).
[P1] When a unique identifier (e.g., MRN) is provided, use it directly to retrieve the patient record and their observations before concluding absence of data.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR), copy the exact phrasing as given, preserving structure and content.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result with a timestamp, compute age relative to current time and act on any time-based conditional.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] When an instruction contains a conditional clause (e.g., 'if X then Y'), ensure both the condition check and the contingent action are completed before finishing.

added_rules: [P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.

action_guidelines: [P1] When searching for a patient by identifier, include both the identifier system and value (e.g., identifier=urn:oid:1.2.3.4|S2823623) or use a known MRN search parameter.
[P1] After retrieving the patient, fetch the most recent HbA1c observation with date to determine if it is older than 1 year.
[P1] If the most recent HbA1c is older than 1 year, create a ServiceRequest for a new HbA1c lab test.
[P1] Return both the HbA1c value and its recorded date as requested.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked potassium (4.3, normal) and correctly skipped replacement, but then ordered a morning serum potassium for next day. The order used 'occurrenceDateTime' instead of a timing extension, causing FHIR validation failure.
reflection.failure_analysis: The order was syntactically valid but semantically incorrect: 'occurrenceDateTime' is not the standard FHIR way to schedule a future lab; a Timing element or a service request with 'occurrencePeriod' or a separate specimen/timing extension is required. The agent failed to use proper FHIR scheduling constructs.
action_guidelines: [P1] When ordering a future lab (e.g., next day at 8am), use a Timing element with a repeat or a period, not occurrenceDateTime alone.
[P1] After any FHIR write action, check the response status code and body for validation errors to confirm the resource was accepted.
[P1] For conditional orders, always complete the full instruction (both condition and independent actions) even if the condition is false.
[P1] When the instruction says 'pair this order with X', treat X as a separate independent order that must be created with its own valid resource.

[P1] Before reporting 'Patient not found', exhaust all search variations: full name, last name only, first name with wildcard, and at least two date format attempts (including swapping day/month and adding/removing leading zeros).
[P1] Do not output any patient-specific value unless the record has been successfully retrieved and verified.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] When asked for patient information by identifier (e.g., MRN), first retrieve the patient record using that identifier.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier and measurement for traceability.
[P1] When an instruction uses 'also' or 'and' after a conditional clause, treat the second clause as an independent action not gated by the condition.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] For time-based decisions, calculate date difference accurately using current timestamp and compare against threshold (e.g., >1 year).
[P1] Before recording any measurement or answering with a specific value, first confirm the patient record exists using the provided identifier.
[P1] When a unique identifier (e.g., MRN) is provided, use it directly to retrieve the patient record and their observations before concluding absence of data.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result with a timestamp, compute age relative to current time and act on any time-based conditional.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence before evaluating thresholds.
[P1] When an instruction contains a conditional clause (e.g., 'if X then Y'), ensure both the condition check and the contingent action are completed before finishing.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked potassium level (3.7, threshold 3.5) and concluded it was not low. It then skipped ordering replacement AND the paired morning lab. The instruction required the morning lab to be ordered regardless of the condition outcome.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent incorrectly gated both actions (replacement + morning lab) on the 'if low' condition. The instruction's 'also' explicitly made the morning lab an independent second action, not conditional on the potassium being low.
action_guidelines: [P1] When an instruction uses 'also' or 'and' after a conditional clause, treat the second clause as an independent action not gated by the condition.
[P1] After a condition is evaluated and the contingent action is skipped, verify whether any remaining instructions (e.g., 'also order X') still need to be performed unconditionally.
[P1] Do not assume a single condition gates all subsequent steps; parse compound instructions into independent actions.

[P1] After retrieving a lab value, explicitly verify its effectiveDateTime falls within the required time window before using it for decision-making.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple independent tasks (e.g., conditional action + unconditional action), execute all tasks independently even if one condition is not met.
[P1] When ordering follow-up or routine lab tests, set priority to 'routine' not 'stat'.
[P1] If no lab exists within the required time window, explicitly state that the condition is not met and return -1 or 'no data'.
[P1] Validate that the measurement value matches expected format (e.g., systolic/diastolic for blood pressure).

[P1] For time-constrained lab queries, filter observation resources by effectiveDateTime to match the specified window (e.g., last 24 hours) before any value-based decision. If no observation matches, return -1 or explicitly state the condition is not met.
[P1] Do not assume the most recent observation satisfies a time constraint unless the query explicitly restricts to that window.
[P1] After recording, confirm the action with patient identifier and measurement for traceability.
[P1] When ordering a lab for a future time, include the scheduled time explicitly in the order.
[P1] When instructed to include a specific free-text note (e.g., SBAR), copy the exact phrasing verbatim without rephrasing.
[P1] When recording a measurement, include patient identifier, value, and timestamp in the confirmation.
[P1] When both name and DOB are provided, use them together in a single search query first to minimize false positives.
[P1] After retrieving a lab value, compute its age relative to current time to decide if re-testing is needed.
[P1] Before finishing, verify each sub-instruction in the original request has been addressed or explicitly explained if skipped.
[P1] Do not assume or fabricate patient demographics; if no record found, return a failure message or request clarification.
[P1] Parse compound instructions for 'if-then-else' logic and ensure each branch is handled explicitly.
[P1] For time-based decisions, calculate date difference accurately using current timestamp and compare against threshold (e.g., >1 year).
[P1] When a unique identifier (e.g., MRN) is provided, use it directly to retrieve the patient record and their observations before concluding absence of data.
[P1] If an action fails, log the exact validation error message to identify missing or incorrect fields.
[P1] If the API returns multiple matches, disambiguate by verifying DOB and full name before selecting the correct patient.
[P1] Return the numeric value of the most recent observation when asked for a specific lab result; avoid adding extraneous context.
[P1] For conditional instructions with an 'if data exists then check value' pattern, explicitly verify data existence before evaluating thresholds.
